philosophy: CONSERVATIVE APPROACH - Zero Risk of Data Loss
principles: NEVER delete original tables until new system is fully validated
Create VIEW layers instead of immediate table consolidation
Gradual migration with fallback to original tables
Preserve all existing API endpoints and functionality
Test each phase thoroughly before proceeding

phases.title: Create Unified Fixtures with VIEW Layer
phases.description: Create unified fixtures table while preserving original tables

steps.action: Create unified fixtures table with sport discriminator
steps.sql:
CREATE TABLE fixtures_unified (
          id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
          match_id VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
          home_team VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
          away_team VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
          venue VARCHAR(200),
          match_date DATE NOT NULL,
          match_time TIME,
          league VARCHAR(100),
          status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'upcoming',
          sport VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'football',
          created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
          UNIQUE(match_id, sport)
        );

steps.action: Migrate football fixtures to unified table
steps.verification: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fixtures_unified WHERE sport = 'football';

steps.action: Migrate cricket fixtures to unified table
steps.verification: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fixtures_unified WHERE sport = 'cricket';

steps.sql:
CREATE VIEW cricket_fixtures_view AS
               SELECT * FROM fixtures_unified WHERE sport = 'cricket';

steps.action: Create service layer for unified access
steps.description: Create service that can query by sport or all fixtures

steps.action: Test cricket functionality with unified table
Gradually update cricket code to use unified table
steps.approach: Add sport parameter to queries, keep original as fallback

phases.title: Unified Rules System with Sport Context
phases.description: Create unified rules table while preserving sport-specific logic

steps.sql:
CREATE TABLE market_rules_unified (
          id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
          tier VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
          sport VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'all',
          rule_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, -- 'volatility', 'markets', 'confidence'
          rule_config JSONB NOT NULL,
          min_confidence DECIMAL(5,2),
          max_confidence DECIMAL(5,2),
          max_predictions INTEGER,
          features JSONB,
          is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
          created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
          updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
        );

INSERT INTO market_rules_unified (tier, sport, rule_type, rule_config, min_confidence, max_confidence, max_predictions, features, created_at)
               SELECT tier, 'all', 'volatility', 
                      jsonb_build_object('allowed_volatility', allowed_volatility),
                      min_confidence, max_confidence, max_predictions, features, created_at
               FROM tier_rules;
steps.verification: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM market_rules_unified WHERE sport = 'all';

steps.sql:
INSERT INTO market_rules_unified (tier, sport, rule_type, rule_config, min_confidence, max_confidence, created_at)
               SELECT tier, 'cricket', 'markets', 
                      jsonb_build_object('allowed_markets', allowed_markets),
                      min_confidence, max_confidence, created_at
               FROM cricket_market_rules;
steps.verification: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM market_rules_unified WHERE sport = 'cricket';

steps.description: Service that queries rules by tier and sport with fallback logic

steps.sql:
CREATE VIEW tier_rules_view AS
               SELECT tier, rule_config->>'allowed_volatility' as allowed_volatility,
                      min_confidence, max_confidence, max_predictions, features, created_at
               FROM market_rules_unified 
               WHERE sport = 'all' AND rule_type = 'volatility' AND is_active = true;

steps.action: Update filter engine to use unified rules
steps.approach: Add sport parameter, maintain backward compatibility

rollbackPlan: All original rule tables remain functional

phases.title: Unified Prediction Pipeline with Status Tracking
phases.description: Create unified predictions table while preserving pipeline stages

steps.action: Create unified predictions table with status tracking
steps.sql:
CREATE TABLE predictions_unified (
          id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
          match_id VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
          home_team VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
          away_team VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
          prediction VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
          confidence DECIMAL(5,2) NOT NULL,
          status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'raw', -- raw, filtered, ai_generated, final
          filter_reason TEXT,
          metadata JSONB,
          ai_model VARCHAR(50),
          sport VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'football',
          market_type VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT '1x2',
          processing_stage VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'stage_1',
          created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
          updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
          INDEX(match_id),
          INDEX(status),
          INDEX(sport),
          INDEX(processing_stage)
        );

steps.verification: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM predictions_unified WHERE status = 'raw';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM predictions_unified WHERE status = 'filtered';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM predictions_unified WHERE status = 'ai_generated';

sql: CREATE VIEW predictions_raw_view AS SELECT * FROM predictions_unified WHERE status = 'raw';
CREATE VIEW predictions_filtered_view AS SELECT * FROM predictions_unified WHERE status = 'filtered';
CREATE VIEW ai_predictions_view AS SELECT * FROM predictions_unified WHERE status = 'ai_generated';

steps.description: Service that handles all prediction stages with status tracking

steps.approach: Add status parameter, keep original tables as fallback

rollbackPlan: All original prediction tables remain functional
validationCriteria: No data loss in any stage

phases.title: Gradual Cleanup and Optimization
phases.description: Remove old tables after thorough validation

steps.action: Monitor system performance with unified tables

steps.filesToUpdate: All remaining files with old table names

steps.action: Remove original tables (OPTIONAL - can keep for safety)

validationCriteria: System runs smoothly without old tables

preMigration: Full database backup before each phase
Export all table structures and data
duringMigration: Keep original tables until new system fully validated
Have rollback scripts ready for each phase
postMigration: Keep backups for at least 30 days
Document all changes for future reference
